medications.indication_rationale: A loop diuretic relieves the pulmonary congestion of decompensated MR, but should be initiated and titrated during in-person assessment with renal/electrolyte monitoring rather than prescribed remotely. The source gave 40 mg orally stat.
medications.dose_route_frequency: furosemide 40 mg orally, single stat dose (as documented in the source in-person encounter; ongoing dosing set on in-person review with monitoring)
contraindications_in_this_case: Requires renal function/electrolyte monitoring; risk of over-diuresis/AKI
Do not initiate/titrate remotely without assessment

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Rest at home with a remote water tablet and a next-week GP review
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Resting hypoxaemia with orthopnoea needs urgent in-person assessment, monitored diuresis and echocardiography; deferral risks acute pulmonary oedema.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Sleep with the head of the bed elevated (or three pillows) and weigh each morning after voiding, seeking review if weight rises quickly.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Upright sleep eases breathing, and daily weights detect fluid retention early.
Sodium restriction reduces fluid retention and supports diuresis.

patient_education_points.point: The valve on the left side of your heart is leaking backward, so your heart is struggling and fluid is backing up into your lungs - that's why you're breathless and can't lie flat. This needs urgent assessment today, not next week.

Please arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment for oxygen if needed, blood tests, a heart tracing and an urgent heart scan (echocardiogram), and referral to a cardiologist about repairing the valve. If your breathing suddenly worsens at rest, you cough up pink frothy sputum, or you get chest pain, call 000.
Sleep propped up tonight, limit salt, and weigh yourself each morning so any fluid build-up is caught early.
Because your symptoms have started, the leaky valve usually needs a specialist assessment for repair or replacement - the urgent scan is the first step.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent same-day in-person assessment; urgent echocardiogram and cardiology/surgical referral; GP review within 48 hours for diuretic response
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Response to diuresis (weight, breathlessness, oxygenation), renal function/electrolytes, echocardiographic MR severity and LV function, timing of valve repair/replacement, and heart-failure management
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Sudden worsening breathlessness at rest, pink frothy sputum, or chest pain - call 000 immediately.

must_include_items: Recognition of decompensated valvular heart failure (mitral regurgitation), not deconditioning
Urgent same-day in-person assessment with echocardiography and cardiology/surgical referral
Explicit statement that safe diuresis and the echocardiogram require in-person assessment
Safety-netting for acute pulmonary oedema (rest dyspnoea, pink frothy sputum, chest pain -> 000)
Head-up sleeping, daily weights and salt restriction
acceptable_alternatives: Urgent ED or acute medical/rapid-access cardiology assessment the same day are acceptable provided echocardiography and monitored diuresis are secured
'Leaky heart valve', 'fluid backing up from a valve problem', 'valvular heart failure' are acceptable framings
errors_of_omission: Attributing decline to ageing/deconditioning with no escalation
errors_of_commission: Reassuring and deferring to a routine appointment
Prescribing a diuretic remotely without assessment
Mislabelling as COPD/asthma and treating with bronchodilators
Ignoring the surgical implication of symptomatic severe MR
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: HIGH-ACUITY CASE: A pass requires (1) recognising decompensated valvular heart failure and (2) urgent same-day in-person assessment with echocardiography and cardiology referral. Deferring to next week or prescribing a remote diuretic without monitoring is a dangerous output given resting hypoxaemia and early pulmonary oedema.
